state_description: Waiting for human approval of the plan
product_description: Habit tracking app with streak management, reminders, statistics, social accountability groups, and integration with Apple Health and Google Fit.

plan.description: Competitive analysis of habit tracking apps (Habitica, Streaks, Finch, Loop, Fabulous). Map feature gaps, monetization models, Apple Health/Google Fit integration patterns, and social accountability mechanics.
acceptance_criteria: Competitive matrix covers ≥6 direct competitors across iOS and Android
Identifies ≥3 unmet user needs in the current market
Documents Apple Health and Google Fit integration approach for top 3 competitors
Includes monetization analysis: freemium vs subscription vs one-time
Defines target market segment and positioning statement

plan.description: Author PRD for habit_tracker: define MVP scope, user personas, MoSCoW feature prioritization, success metrics (DAU, streak retention, D7/D30 retention), and phased roadmap across MVP → Social Layer → Health Integration → AI Coaching.
acceptance_criteria: PRD defines ≥3 user personas with jobs-to-be-done
MoSCoW feature list with clear MVP boundary documented
Success metrics defined: DAU, D7 retention, D30 retention, streak completion rate
Phased roadmap with milestones per phase approved by stakeholders
API integration requirements documented for Apple Health (HealthKit) and Google Fit (Health Connect)

plan.description: User research (5 interviews), affinity mapping, information architecture, and high-fidelity wireframes for all core screens: onboarding, habit list, streak calendar heatmap, reminder config, statistics, social group feed, health sync settings. WCAG 2.1 AA audit.

screens: Apple Health / Google Fit sync settings

acceptance_criteria: ≥5 user research interviews completed with affinity map
Complete information architecture diagram approved
High-fidelity Figma prototype covering all core screens
Usability test with ≥5 participants, ≥80% task completion rate
WCAG 2.1 AA audit passed: color contrast, touch targets ≥44px, screen reader labels
Streak calendar heatmap visualisation validated with users
App store screenshot mockups prepared
Onboarding flow under 3 steps

plan.description: Design PostgreSQL 16 schema: users, habits, habit_completions, streaks, reminders, social_groups, group_memberships, group_posts, health_sync_configs, audit_log. Define indexes, FK constraints, soft-delete patterns, and Alembic migration scripts.

payload.security: health sync tokens AES-256 column-level encryption

acceptance_criteria: ERD reviewed and approved by engineering lead
All FK constraints and indexes defined
Soft-delete (deleted_at/archived_at) applied to user-facing entities
Alembic migration scripts generate clean schema from empty DB
Streak query (last 30 days completions) executes in <10ms on 100k rows
Health sync tokens encrypted at rest (column-level AES-256)

plan.description: Design OpenAPI 3.1 REST API contract for all habit_tracker endpoints: auth, habits CRUD, completions, streaks, reminders, social groups, health sync. Define JWT RS256 auth strategy, rate limits, RFC 7807 error schema, and cursor-based pagination.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAPI 3.1 spec passes spectral lint with zero errors
All endpoints have request/response schemas with examples
Error responses follow RFC 7807 Problem Details format
Auth flow documented: register → login → JWT → refresh → logout
Health sync OAuth2 PKCE callback flow documented for HealthKit and Health Connect
Contract signed off by frontend and backend leads

plan.description: STRIDE threat model on auth, social group access control, and health data handling. Define controls: HTTPS enforcement, JWT validation, RBAC for group roles, rate limiting, input sanitization, OWASP Top 10 checklist. Health data compliance review vs HealthKit and Health Connect policies.
acceptance_criteria: STRIDE threat model completed for all 5 API surface areas
OWASP Top 10 checklist completed with mitigations
Health data handling complies with Apple HealthKit and Google Health Connect privacy policies
RBAC matrix defined: group owner, member, admin roles with permission matrix
Data retention and deletion policy documented (GDPR right to erasure)
Health OAuth tokens stored encrypted; refresh tokens in httpOnly cookies

plan.description: Draft Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, and Data Processing Agreement covering health data (HealthKit, Health Connect), social features, UGC in groups, and account deletion. Ensure GDPR Article 17, CCPA, and Apple/Google platform policies compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy Policy covers all data types collected including health data
ToS includes acceptable use policy for social groups
GDPR data subject rights described: access, erasure, portability
HealthKit and Health Connect data usage restrictions clearly stated
Data Processing Agreement template available for B2B use
Reviewed by legal counsel before publishing

plan.description: Build compliance traceability matrix mapping PRD requirements to implementation tasks and test cases. Define health data handling procedures aligned with Apple HealthKit guidelines and Google Health Connect policy. Establish audit log specification.
acceptance_criteria: Every PRD requirement traces to ≥1 implementation task and ≥1 test case
Health data flow diagram shows complete path from HealthKit/Health Connect to DB
Audit log captures: user ID, action, resource, timestamp for all data mutations
Data retention schedule defined per data type
Health data never stored in cleartext logs or analytics events

plan.description: Provision AWS cloud infrastructure via Terraform: EKS cluster, RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7, S3+CloudFront for media assets, SES for email, SNS/FCM bridge for push notifications, Secrets Manager, VPC with private subnets. Three environments: dev/staging/prod.
acceptance_criteria: Terraform modules pass `terraform plan` with zero errors for all 3 environments
RDS in private subnet, accessible only from EKS worker nodes
Redis TLS enabled with AUTH required
S3 blocks public access; CloudFront uses signed URLs for private assets
Secrets Manager rotation configured for DB credentials and JWT signing keys
RDS automated backups: 7-day retention, point-in-time restore tested

plan.description: Configure SAGE habit_tracker solution using Multi-Agent Coordinator pattern. Set up three specialist agents: reminder-intelligence (optimal send-time via ReAct), streak-coach (motivational nudges at risk points), social-engagement (group activity summarization). Author project.yaml, prompts.yaml, tasks.yaml.
acceptance_criteria: project.yaml defines solution name, active_modules, theme, and coordinator config
prompts.yaml contains system prompts for all 3 specialist agents
tasks.yaml defines task types: reminder_optimization, streak_coaching, group_engagement
SAGE loads habit_tracker solution without errors: `make run PROJECT=habit_tracker`
Coordinator routes tasks to correct specialist agent by task_type

plan.description: Implement FastAPI backend: auth service (JWT RS256 + refresh rotation), habits CRUD, streak calculation engine (daily/weekly/custom frequencies, freeze logic), reminder scheduler (APScheduler + Redis), social groups service, push notification dispatcher (FCM + APNs), Celery async workers.
acceptance_criteria: All OpenAPI spec endpoints implemented with correct response schemas
StreakCalculator handles: daily habits, weekly habits, custom days, freeze/forgiveness, longest streak
Reminder scheduler fires within ±60 seconds of scheduled time
Group invitation codes are cryptographically random (secrets.token_urlsafe)
Health sync OAuth2 PKCE flow completes end-to-end for both HealthKit and Health Connect
Push notifications delivered via FCM (Android) and APNs (iOS) with delivery receipts logged
`make test` passes with ≥80% line coverage on streak and auth modules

plan.description: Implement BLE companion firmware for optional wearable habit-tracker band: STM32L4 MCU, FreeRTOS 10.6, Nordic SoftDevice BLE, haptic feedback driver (DRV2605L) for reminder alerts and streak milestones, accelerometer (LIS2DH12) step detection, GATT server for habit completion offload.
acceptance_criteria: Firmware compiles clean (zero warnings -Wall -Wextra) for STM32L4 target
BLE GATT profile exposes: habit_completion write characteristic, reminder_alert notify characteristic
Haptic patterns: short pulse 100ms (reminder), long pulse 500ms (streak milestone)
Sleep current <15µA measured with multimeter in series
Step detection accuracy ≥90% vs pedometer ground truth over 1000-step test
OTA firmware update via nRF DFU functional on physical hardware

plan.description: Design PCB schematic and layout for habit-tracker wearable band in KiCad 8: STM32L4 + nRF52840, BQ25185 LiPo charger, DRV2605L haptic driver, LIS2DH12 accelerometer, USB-C charging. 4-layer stackup, 38×18mm wrist-form-factor. Generate Gerbers and BOM.
acceptance_criteria: Schematic ERC passes with zero errors in KiCad
PCB layout DRC passes with zero errors at 0.1mm minimum clearance
BOM generated with LCSC part numbers for all components
Gerbers pass JLCPCB DFM check
Power trace width ≥0.5mm for charging path, ≥0.25mm for logic signals
BLE antenna clearance meets Nordic reference design keep-out zone guidelines

plan.description: LTspice SPICE model of BQ25185 CC/CV charging circuit and SystemC power model of FreeRTOS task duty cycle to validate battery life (≥5 days), charge time (≤1 hour), and sleep current (<15µA) before PCB fabrication.

simulations: SPICE: BQ25185 CC/CV charge cycle for 150mAh at 500mA — validate charge time
SPICE: LDO dropout at low battery (3.0V) — verify MCU rail stability
SystemC: FreeRTOS BLE advertising + sleep duty cycle — daily battery life estimate
SystemC: Peak TX current (7mA BLE) — LDO output ripple <50mV

acceptance_criteria: SPICE charge simulation completes CC/CV within ≤1.0 hours for 150mAh cell
SystemC model estimates ≥5 days battery life under normal usage (6 reminders/day)
Sleep current simulation confirms <15µA in deep sleep
LDO output ripple <50mV under peak BLE TX current
Results documented in power_budget_analysis.pdf
Simulation values within 10% of prototype measurements post-fabrication

plan.description: Implement cross-platform mobile app in React Native 0.74 + Expo SDK 51: onboarding, habit list, streak calendar heatmap (52-week), habit creation wizard, statistics charts (Victory Native), social group feed, reminder configuration, Apple Health (react-native-health) and Google Fit (react-native-google-fit) sync screens.
acceptance_criteria: App builds and runs on iOS 16+ and Android 12+ without crashes
Habit list loads within 300ms on Pixel 6
Calendar heatmap renders 52 weeks of completions with correct color gradient
Reminder notifications fire at scheduled time (±60s) when app is backgrounded
HealthKit sync reads step count and writes habit completions as mindful sessions
Health Connect sync reads steps and writes habit completion events
Social group feed loads with infinite scroll, <500ms initial paint
All screens match Figma designs within ±2px / ±2% color tolerance

plan.description: CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions: backend (ruff → mypy → pytest → Docker → EKS rolling deploy) and mobile (tsc → Jest → Expo EAS Build → TestFlight + Play Internal). Monitoring: Datadog APM, Sentry crash reporting, PagerDuty alerting on P95 > 200ms or error rate > 1%.
acceptance_criteria: Backend pipeline completes in <8 minutes for a standard PR
EAS Build produces signed IPA and APK in <15 minutes
Zero-downtime rolling deployment to EKS (maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0)
Sentry alerts fire within 2 minutes of a new crash
Datadog dashboard shows API latency, error rate, active users
Rollback procedure documented and tested: manual tag rollback <5 minutes

plan.description: Write pytest unit and integration test suite for backend: auth flows, streak calculator edge cases (timezone boundaries, freeze logic, longest streak), reminder scheduler, social group RBAC, health sync OAuth PKCE flow, push notification dispatch. Target ≥85% line coverage.

test_categories: streak (daily, weekly, timezone midnight, freeze, longest)
payload.db_strategy: real PostgreSQL in Docker Compose (no DB mocks)

acceptance_criteria: ≥85% line coverage across src/ (excluding migrations and fixtures)
Streak tests cover: 30-day daily streak, weekly habit, timezone midnight boundary, freeze, longest streak
Auth tests cover: JWT expiry, refresh token rotation, concurrent session limit
All tests pass in <120 seconds in CI
Zero flaky tests (no sleep() calls, all async properly awaited)
Integration tests hit real PostgreSQL — no DB mocks

plan.description: Firmware unit tests (Unity + CMock) and HIL test specification for wearable: BLE GATT write/notify validation, haptic pattern timing (oscilloscope), accelerometer step detection accuracy (1000-step walk), RTC alarm accuracy (24hr soak), sleep current measurement, OTA DFU flow.

test_cases: BLE GATT: write habit_completion characteristic → audit log entry created

Accelerometer: ≥90% step detection accuracy over 1000-step walk

Power: sleep current <15µA measured with multimeter in series
OTA DFU: firmware update via nRF Connect completes without brick

acceptance_criteria: Unity unit test suite passes 100% on target hardware
HIL test report documents pass/fail for all 7 test categories
BLE GATT notify latency P95 <100ms measured over 100 trials
Step detection accuracy ≥90% in 3 independent 1000-step walks
RTC alarm accuracy ±5s confirmed over 24-hour soak
Sleep current ≤15µA confirmed on 3 prototype units
OTA DFU succeeds on 10 consecutive update cycles

plan.description: QA test plan for habit_tracker mobile app: smoke (20 cases), regression (150 cases), health sync (30 cases), social (40 cases), and exploratory charter for edge cases (streak midnight boundary, timezone change mid-streak, concurrent group actions). Device matrix: 8 devices across iOS 16/17/18 and Android 12/13/14.

test_suites.smoke: 20 cases — install, register, create habit, complete, check streak
test_suites.regression: 150 cases — all features, error states, offline mode
test_suites.health_sync: 30 cases — HealthKit and Health Connect grant/revoke/conflict
test_suites.social: 40 cases — group create, join, post, accountability nudge
test_suites.exploratory: midnight streak boundary, timezone change, concurrent actions

acceptance_criteria: Test plan reviewed and approved before any release candidate
Smoke suite passes 100% on all 8 devices before beta release
Regression suite passes ≥98% before production release
Health sync tested on physical iPhone (TestFlight) and Pixel (real Health Connect)
Zero P1 (crash/data loss) bugs open at release gate
Bug severity classification documented: P1=crash/data loss, P2=feature broken, P3=cosmetic

plan.description: End-to-end system tests: full user journeys via Detox (React Native E2E on iOS Simulator + Android Emulator), load testing at 10k concurrent users via k6, and health sync integration against live HealthKit/Health Connect sandboxes on physical devices.

e2e_scenarios: register → create habit → complete → streak → reminder → notification
create group → invite → both complete → see group feed
enable HealthKit → complete habit → verify mindful session written
miss a day → re-engage → verify streak reset

acceptance_criteria: All 5 E2E scenarios pass on iOS Simulator (iPhone 15 Pro, iOS 17) and Android Emulator (Pixel 6, API 33)
Load test: P95 <200ms, P99 <500ms, error rate <0.1% at 10k concurrent users
Streak recalculation for 5,000 simultaneous completions completes in <30 seconds
Health sync E2E verified on physical device (TestFlight) against real HealthKit sandbox
Account deletion E2E confirms zero DB rows for deleted user after purge job

plan.description: Set up i18n infrastructure (i18n-js + expo-localization for mobile, Babel for backend email/push templates). Implement locale-aware date formatting for streak heatmap, 12h/24h reminder display, week start day. Initial translations: English, Spanish, French, Portuguese (BR).

payload.backend_i18n: Babel (Python) for email and push notification templates

acceptance_criteria: All user-visible strings extracted to translation files (zero hardcoded strings)
Spanish, French, Portuguese (BR) translations complete and reviewed by native speakers
Date and time display respects device locale on iOS and Android
Week start day uses locale default and is user-overridable in settings
Push notification copy delivered in user's app language
Pluralization rules correct for all 4 locales
Top 5 target locales identified
Store listing translated

plan.description: Create user-facing documentation and onboarding materials: 5-step in-app interactive tutorial, 20-article help center (Notion/Zendesk), App Store product page copy (iOS + Android), and 3-minute onboarding video script. All materials localized for 4 supported locales.

deliverables: App Store listing (title, subtitle, description, keywords, screenshots)
FAQ top 10 (streak freeze, health sync troubleshooting, group privacy)

acceptance_criteria: In-app tutorial completes without errors on iOS and Android
Help center covers all 20 feature areas with search working
App Store description within 4,000 character limit and passes review guidelines
Onboarding video reviewed and approved by product manager
All materials pass Flesch-Kincaid readability test at ≤8th grade level
Training materials localized for all 4 supported locales

plan.description: Write technical documentation: Redoc API reference (auto-generated from OpenAPI 3.1), backend architecture README with system diagram, deployment runbook, schemaspy-generated database ERD, health sync integration guide for partners, and CHANGELOG in kept-a-changelog format.

deliverables: Health Sync Integration Guide (HealthKit + Health Connect OAuth setup)
payload.changelog_enforcement: CI check ensures CHANGELOG updated on every release PR

acceptance_criteria: Redoc API reference renders all endpoints with request/response examples
Architecture diagram reviewed by all team leads
Deployment runbook validated by running prod deploy from scratch following only the doc
Database ERD reflects current migration state (auto-generated, not hand-drawn)
Health sync guide enables a developer to implement integration in <4 hours
CHANGELOG update enforced as CI check on release PRs

flaws: HealthKit does NOT use OAuth2 PKCE. HealthKit uses HKHealthStore.requestAuthorization() — a native iOS permission dialog with no callback URL or authorization code. The plan documents 'Health sync OAuth2 PKCE callback flow for HealthKit' (steps 5, 6, 11) which is architecturally wrong and will require rework across API design, backend, and security model.

Steps 12-13 introduce a dual-MCU design (STM32L476RG + nRF52840) but treat it as a single firmware target. These are two physically separate chips requiring separate firmware images, an inter-chip communication protocol (SPI or UART), and synchronized state machines. Step 12 only targets STM32L4 with Nordic SoftDevice — SoftDevice runs on nRF, not STM32. This is a fundamental hardware architecture contradiction.

Offline mode appears only in QA regression scope ('150 cases — all features, error states, offline mode') with zero implementation steps. A habit tracking app must work offline — users complete habits in planes, gyms, and subways. No local SQLite, no sync conflict resolution, no optimistic UI, no queue-and-sync pattern is specified anywhere in the build steps.

No monetization implementation exists. Step 1 analyzes pricing models; the PRD (step 2) mentions freemium vs subscription; but there is no implementation step for StoreKit 2 (iOS in-app purchases), Google Play Billing, subscription state management, paywall UI, entitlement checking, or receipt validation. A consumer app without a monetization implementation path cannot generate revenue.

Step 11 depends on Step 10 (SAGE agent config), meaning the core FastAPI backend cannot be built until AI agent YAML files are authored. This dependency is inverted — SAGE agents call the backend API, not the reverse. This blocks the critical path unnecessarily.

GDPR right to erasure is inconsistent: the legal step (7) states 'account deletion within 30 days' (async background job) but the E2E test (step 20) validates 'account deletion → verify data purged' immediately. The architecture needs a defined deletion model: soft-delete immediately, hard-delete by scheduled purge job within 30 days, with user-visible status.

No product analytics implementation. The north star metric is '30-day streak retention rate' but there is no Mixpanel, Amplitude, Firebase Analytics, or PostHog integration step. You cannot measure DAU, D7, D30, or streak retention without an event tracking pipeline. This makes the success metrics in the PRD unmeasurable.

FCC Part 15 + CE RED certification for BLE hardware is listed as 'planned' in the PCB step but has no corresponding implementation step, budget estimate, or timeline allocation. FCC certification alone takes 4-12 weeks and $15,000-$30,000 minimum. This is not a checkbox — it is a regulatory gate that blocks hardware shipping.

Step 20 system test load target (10,000 concurrent users, 1,000 RPS) has no corresponding EKS cluster sizing, RDS instance class specification, or Redis cache sizing. Running k6 at 1,000 RPS against an undersized cluster will produce meaningless results and false failures at test time.

The 'writing habit completions as mindful sessions' to HealthKit (step 15 acceptance criteria) is an architectural assumption that Apple may reject during App Store review. Apple scrutinizes HealthKit data type usage — writing habit completions as mindful sessions is a misuse of that category. This needs explicit validation against HealthKit developer guidelines before implementation.
suggestions: Replace 'Health sync OAuth2 PKCE flow for HealthKit' with 'HKHealthStore.requestAuthorization() permission flow' across steps 5, 6, 11, and 15. Health Connect (Android) does use OAuth2; keep that. Audit every acceptance criterion that mentions HealthKit OAuth.

Split the wearable into a separate product workstream or explicitly gate it behind a post-MVP milestone. Steps 12-14 add 4-6 months of specialized embedded work, certification overhead, and supply chain risk with zero validated demand. Ship the app first.

Add a dedicated OFFLINE step between steps 11 and 15: define the local-first data model (WatermelonDB or expo-sqlite), optimistic completion writes, background sync with conflict resolution (last-write-wins or server-authoritative), and connectivity state indicators in UI.

Add a MONETIZATION step after step 2: StoreKit 2 integration (iOS), Google Play Billing 5 (Android), RevenueCat as the abstraction layer, subscription entitlement checking middleware in the FastAPI backend, and paywall screen in the frontend.
Move Step 10 (SAGE config) out of the critical path for Step 11. SAGE agents should be configured against a running backend, not as a prerequisite for building it. Step 10 can depend on step 11.

Add a PUSH_INFRASTRUCTURE step between steps 9 and 11: Firebase project setup, APNs certificate provisioning, device token registration endpoint, token refresh strategy, notification category definitions for iOS (actionable notifications), and opted-out user handling.

Define the timezone strategy for streak calculation in the PRD (step 2) not just as a QA edge case (step 19). Decision: does 'today' mean user local midnight or UTC midnight? What happens on DST transitions? What happens when a user changes their device timezone mid-streak? This is a data model decision, not a test case.

Add a CONTENT_MODERATION step for social features: abuse reporting endpoint, automated toxicity screening (Perspective API or equivalent), moderator review queue, and ban/mute mechanics. Social UGC without moderation creates platform liability.

Replace EKS with ECS Fargate for the initial deployment. EKS adds Kubernetes operational overhead (node groups, cluster autoscaler, control plane management) that is disproportionate for an unproven consumer app. Migrate to EKS when sustained load justifies it.

Add an APP_STORE_READINESS step before DevOps (step 16): App Store Connect setup, privacy nutrition labels, HealthKit usage justification strings, App Review information, TestFlight external test group creation, and Play Console setup with Health Connect permissions declaration.
missing: Offline-first architecture and sync conflict resolution — not mentioned in any implementation step
In-app purchase / subscription implementation (StoreKit 2, Google Play Billing, RevenueCat)
Product analytics event tracking pipeline (required to measure the north star metric)
Push notification infrastructure setup (Firebase, APNs certificates, token lifecycle)
nRF52840 firmware specification — the BLE SoC has no firmware plan despite being on the PCB schematic
Inter-chip communication protocol between STM32L4 and nRF52840 (SPI/UART framing, command set)
FCC/CE certification workstream with timeline and cost estimate
App Store Connect and Google Play Console setup and submission workflow
Streak timezone strategy — a business logic decision missing from the PRD
Social content moderation system (reporting, review queue, enforcement)
Data export feature (GDPR Article 20 portability — mentioned legally but not implemented)
Database zero-downtime migration strategy for live production schema changes
Rate limiting implementation location (API gateway vs. application layer vs. Redis)
Background fetch / background app refresh entitlements for iOS reminder reliability
security_risks: HealthKit data written to push notification payloads or analytics events would violate HealthKit guidelines and potentially GDPR. Step 8 mentions 'health data never stored in cleartext logs or analytics events' as a compliance criterion but no enforcement mechanism (log scrubbing, PII filtering middleware) is specified in the implementation steps.

Group invitation codes: the plan specifies secrets.token_urlsafe (correct) but does not specify code expiry, single-use enforcement, or rate limiting on the join endpoint. A non-expiring group code is a permanent membership escalation vector.

JWT RS256 private key storage: Secrets Manager rotation is mentioned for DB credentials and JWT signing keys (step 9) but key rotation for RS256 requires coordinated key rollover — existing valid tokens must remain verifiable during rotation. No key versioning or rotation strategy is specified, risking authentication outages on key rotation.

Health OAuth tokens (Health Connect) stored encrypted at column level — but the encryption key management is unspecified beyond 'AES-256'. Who holds the key? Is it in Secrets Manager? Is it per-user or global? A global encryption key means a single key compromise decrypts all health tokens.

Social group posts (UGC) with no input length limits or content-type restrictions in the API spec create a stored XSS and database bloat risk. The OpenAPI spec step (5) does not mention max payload sizes for group_posts.

APNs device tokens stored in the database — if the users table is exfiltrated, attackers can send arbitrary push notifications to all users (push phishing). Device tokens should be stored in a separate table with restricted access, not inline in user records.

Refresh token rotation without concurrent session invalidation: if the plan allows multiple active refresh tokens (common for multi-device use), a stolen refresh token remains valid until it is used. The acceptance criteria mention 'concurrent session limit' in tests but the implementation step does not define the session model.
final_review.summary: This plan is architecturally ambitious but contains one concrete technical error that will cause production rework (HealthKit OAuth2 PKCE does not exist), one hardware design contradiction that blocks firmware delivery (dual-MCU with single firmware target), and two major feature gaps that affect core product viability (offline mode for a habit app, no monetization implementation). The software-only portions of the plan (steps 1-11, 15-23) are well-structured with real database integration tests, proper security tooling, and a defensible tech stack. However, the wearable hardware workstream (steps 12-14) is underspecified, lacks certification timelines, and introduces firmware complexity that is disproportionate to an unvalidated consumer product. The plan also conflates MVP with full-product delivery — steps 12-23 represent 6+ months of additional work beyond a functional app. Scoring 52: the software foundation is sound but the HealthKit misconception is a concrete bug in the design, offline mode is a product-defining omission for a habit tracker, and the hardware workstream has blocking technical contradictions requiring rework before any fabrication.

These are two physically separate chips requiring separate firmware images, an inter-chip communication protocol (SPI or UART), and synchronized state machines. Step 12 only targets STM32L4 with Nordic SoftDevice — SoftDevice runs on nRF, not STM32. This is a fundamental hardware architecture contradiction.

A habit tracking app must work offline — users complete habits in planes, gyms, and subways. A consumer app without a monetization implementation path cannot generate revenue.

The architecture needs a defined deletion model: soft-delete immediately, hard-delete by scheduled purge job within 30 days, with user-visible status.

You cannot measure DAU, D7, D30, or streak retention without an event tracking pipeline. This makes the success metrics in the PRD unmeasurable.

FCC certification alone takes 4-12 weeks and $15,000-$30,000 minimum. This is not a checkbox — it is a regulatory gate that blocks hardware shipping.

This needs explicit validation against HealthKit developer guidelines before implementation.

What happens on DST transitions? What happens when a user changes their device timezone mid-streak? This is a data model decision, not a test case.

Add a CONTENT_MODERATION step for social features: abuse reporting endpoint, automated toxicity screening (Perspective API or equivalent), moderator review queue, and ban/mute mechanics. Social UGC without moderation creates platform liability.

Replace EKS with ECS Fargate for the initial deployment. EKS adds Kubernetes operational overhead (node groups, cluster autoscaler, control plane management) that is disproportionate for an unproven consumer app. Migrate to EKS when sustained load justifies it.

Add an APP_STORE_READINESS step before DevOps (step 16): App Store Connect setup, privacy nutrition labels, HealthKit usage justification strings, App Review information, TestFlight external test group creation, and Play Console setup with Health Connect permissions declaration.
missing: Offline-first architecture and sync conflict resolution — not mentioned in any implementation step
In-app purchase / subscription implementation (StoreKit 2, Google Play Billing, RevenueCat)
Product analytics event tracking pipeline (required to measure the north star metric)
Push notification infrastructure setup (Firebase, APNs certificates, token lifecycle)
nRF52840 firmware specification — the BLE SoC has no firmware plan despite being on the PCB schematic
Inter-chip communication protocol between STM32L4 and nRF52840 (SPI/UART framing, command set)
FCC/CE certification workstream with timeline and cost estimate
App Store Connect and Google Play Console setup and submission workflow
Streak timezone strategy — a business logic decision missing from the PRD
Social content moderation system (reporting, review queue, enforcement)
Data export feature (GDPR Article 20 portability — mentioned legally but not implemented)
Database zero-downtime migration strategy for live production schema changes
Rate limiting implementation location (API gateway vs. application layer vs. Redis)
Background fetch / background app refresh entitlements for iOS reminder reliability
security_risks: HealthKit data written to push notification payloads or analytics events would violate HealthKit guidelines and potentially GDPR. Step 8 mentions 'health data never stored in cleartext logs or analytics events' as a compliance criterion but no enforcement mechanism (log scrubbing, PII filtering middleware) is specified in the implementation steps.

Group invitation codes: the plan specifies secrets.token_urlsafe (correct) but does not specify code expiry, single-use enforcement, or rate limiting on the join endpoint. A non-expiring group code is a permanent membership escalation vector.

JWT RS256 private key storage: Secrets Manager rotation is mentioned for DB credentials and JWT signing keys (step 9) but key rotation for RS256 requires coordinated key rollover — existing valid tokens must remain verifiable during rotation. No key versioning or rotation strategy is specified, risking authentication outages on key rotation.

Health OAuth tokens (Health Connect) stored encrypted at column level — but the encryption key management is unspecified beyond 'AES-256'. Who holds the key? Is it in Secrets Manager? Is it per-user or global? A global encryption key means a single key compromise decrypts all health tokens.

Social group posts (UGC) with no input length limits or content-type restrictions in the API spec create a stored XSS and database bloat risk. The OpenAPI spec step (5) does not mention max payload sizes for group_posts.

APNs device tokens stored in the database — if the users table is exfiltrated, attackers can send arbitrary push notifications to all users (push phishing). Device tokens should be stored in a separate table with restricted access, not inline in user records.

Refresh token rotation without concurrent session invalidation: if the plan allows multiple active refresh tokens (common for multi-device use), a stolen refresh token remains valid until it is used. The acceptance criteria mention 'concurrent session limit' in tests but the implementation step does not define the session model.
history.summary: This plan is architecturally ambitious but contains one concrete technical error that will cause production rework (HealthKit OAuth2 PKCE does not exist), one hardware design contradiction that blocks firmware delivery (dual-MCU with single firmware target), and two major feature gaps that affect core product viability (offline mode for a habit app, no monetization implementation). The software-only portions of the plan (steps 1-11, 15-23) are well-structured with real database integration tests, proper security tooling, and a defensible tech stack. However, the wearable hardware workstream (steps 12-14) is underspecified, lacks certification timelines, and introduces firmware complexity that is disproportionate to an unvalidated consumer product. The plan also conflates MVP with full-product delivery — steps 12-23 represent 6+ months of additional work beyond a functional app. Scoring 52: the software foundation is sound but the HealthKit misconception is a concrete bug in the design, offline mode is a product-defining omission for a habit tracker, and the hardware workstream has blocking technical contradictions requiring rework before any fabrication.
